perturbation.original_text: (a) The term “ affiliates ” shall mean, with respect to any Person, any other Person directly or indirectly controlling, controlled by, or under common control with such Person.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) The term “ affiliates ” shall mean, with respect to any Person, any other Person directly or indirectly related to, associated with, or collaborating with such Person.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by broadening the definition of 'affiliates' to include entities that are merely 'related to, associated with, or collaborating with' a Person, instead of entities under direct or indirect control. This contradicts other contract sections (like Section 9 regarding Standstill obligations) that rely on the original definition of control to determine the scope of restrictions, leading to uncertainty about which entities are bound by these restrictions. This uncertainty could significantly impact the enforceability of the standstill provision.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Participant agrees that, for a period of one year after the date of this Agreement (the “ Standstill Period ”), unless specifically invited in writing by the Company, neither it nor (1) any of its majority-owned subsidiaries, (2) its Representatives (acting on its behalf), or (3) Roark Capital Acquisition LLC or its affiliates who receive Evaluation Material directly from Roark Capital Acquisition LLC or Participant or at their direction, will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in, or in any way assist any other Person to effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in: (i) any acquisition of any securities (or any acquisition of Beneficial Ownership thereof) or assets of the Company or any of its subsidiaries, (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving the Company or any of its subsidiaries, (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to the Company or any of its subsidiaries, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of the Company; (b) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (as defined under the Act) with respect to the securities of the Company; (c) make any public announcement with respect to, or submit an unsolicited proposal for or offer of (with or without condition), any extraordinary transaction involving the Company or its securities or assets; (d) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of the Company; (e) take any action which may reasonably be expected to force the Company to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in (a) above; or (f) enter into any discussions or arrangements with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of 'affiliates' from entities 'controlled by' to entities 'related to, associated with, or collaborating with' a Person. The contradicted text (Standstill obligations) restricts certain actions by the Participant and its 'affiliates.' Because the definition of affiliates has been broadened to include entities merely 'related to' the participant, entities who were previously not considered affiliates are now included in that definition, and thus bound by the Standstill obligations, despite not being 'controlled by' the Participant. This creates a contradiction because the Standstill agreement was intended to bind only those entities controlled by the Participant and has now been broadened to entities that aren't, thus creating a strong contradiction with the intention of the Contradicted Text. The change directly impacts the scope and enforceability of the standstill provision. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Each Recipient shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, (i) use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction and (ii) keep the Evaluation Material confidential, and, subject to Section 5, will not, and will cause its Representatives not to, disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever
perturbation.changed_text: Each Recipient shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, (i) use the Evaluation Material for any legitimate business purpose and (ii) keep the Evaluation Material confidential, and, subject to Section 5, will not, and will cause its Representatives not to, disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the permitted use of the Evaluation Material from 'solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction' to 'for any legitimate business purpose'. This contradicts the purpose explicitly stated and agreed upon in the original text, because 'any legitimate business purpose' is more broad than the original agreement. This creates uncertainty, as the original intent was to restrict Evaluation Material to evaluate a Possible Transaction, and not for potentially exploiting such information for other means. Such Exploitation is now a possibility, which creates an in-text contradiction. If the evaluation material is used for means beyond the scope of the Possible transaction, this can lead to disputes over contractual obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Recipient shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, (i) use the Evaluation Material for any legitimate business purpose and (ii) keep the Evaluation Material confidential, and, subject to Section 5, will not, and will cause its Representatives not to, disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however , that any of such information may be disclosed to Recipient’s Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of helping Recipient evaluate a Possible Transaction. Each Recipient agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of such Recipient’s Representatives. This Agreement does not grant Recipient or any of its Representatives any license to use Provider’s Evaluation Material except as provided herein. In addition, all proprietary and intellectual property rights in and to the Evaluation Material shall remain the sole property of Provider, and nothing in this Agreement shall be construed in any way to grant to Recipient or its Representatives or any other Person any express or implied option, license or other right, title or interest in or to any Evaluation Material, or to any intellectual property rights embodied in such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the permitted use of the Evaluation Material to 'any legitimate business purpose' from the original 'solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction.' The contradicted text states: 'Each Recipient shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, (i) use the Evaluation Material for any legitimate business purpose and (ii) keep the Evaluation Material confidential... provided, however , that any of such information may be disclosed to Recipient’s Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of helping Recipient evaluate a Possible Transaction'. While the changed text and contradicted text are similar, the changed text contradicts the original intent of using the evaluation material solely for the possible transaction, creating an inconsistency regarding its permitted use, which creates an in-text contradiction according to the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: At any time upon the request of a Provider for any reason, a Recipient will, and will direct its Representatives to, promptly, but not later than 10 business days, after receipt of such notice or request, destroy or return (at Recipient’s option) all Evaluation Material and all documents that incorporate Evaluation Material, and no copy or extract thereof (including electronic copies) shall be retained, except that Recipient and its Representatives may retain one copy to be kept confidential and used solely for archival, legal, and compliance purposes.
perturbation.changed_text: At any time upon the request of a Provider for a material breach of this agreement, a Recipient will, and will direct its Representatives to, promptly, but not later than 30 business days, after receipt of such notice or request, destroy or return (at Recipient’s option) all Evaluation Material and all documents that incorporate Evaluation Material, and copies can be retained by the Recipient and its Representatives for any purpose.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction concerning the conditions and timeline for returning or destroying evaluation material and on the retention of the data itself. The original text states that the material should be destroyed within 10 days for any reason with one copy for archival, legal, and compliance purposes. The modified version changes the return condition to 'a material breach', and lengthens the return time to 30 days, and allows the copies to be retained for 'any purpose'. This contradicts the restrictions placed earlier in the original agreement. This directly contradicts section 3 of the agreement in regards to the use of Evaluation Material
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Recipient shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, (i) use the Evaluation Material for any legitimate business purpose and (ii) keep the Evaluation Material confidential, and, subject to Section 5, will not, and will cause its Representatives not to, disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however , that any of such information may be disclosed to Recipient’s Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of helping Recipient evaluate a Possible Transaction. Each Recipient agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of such Recipient’s Representatives. This Agreement does not grant Recipient or any of its Representatives any license to use Provider’s Evaluation Material except as provided herein. In addition, all proprietary and intellectual property rights in and to the Evaluation Material shall remain the sole property of Provider, and nothing in this Agreement shall be construed in any way to grant to Recipient or its Representatives or any other Person any express or implied option, license or other right, title or interest in or to any Evaluation Material, or to any intellectual property rights embodied in such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction with the contradicted text. The original text mandates the return or destruction of evaluation material within 10 days for any reason, retaining only one copy for archival, legal, and compliance purposes. The modified version changes the condition to 'a material breach', extends the deadline to 30 days, and allows copies to be retained for 'any purpose.' This directly contradicts the contradicted section which states that the Evaluation Material should be used for a legitimate business purpose. The changed text now allows retention for 'any purpose' and directly impacts the use of the evaluation material as restricted in the contradicted text which limits its use.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists between the Parties unless and until a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, and each Party hereby waives, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) relating to the existence of a Possible Transaction unless and until both Parties shall have entered into a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party understands and agrees that a preliminary agreement providing for a Possible Transaction is established upon the exchange of Evaluation Material, implying a good faith intention to negotiate a final definitive agreement. Each Party acknowledges that this preliminary agreement creates a non-binding obligation to proceed with negotiations in good faith, subject to the terms and conditions herein.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that a preliminary agreement exists upon the exchange of Evaluation Material. This directly contradicts the original clause which clearly states that 'no contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists unless and until a final definitive agreement has been executed'. The modified text creates uncertainty about the enforceability of the 'preliminary agreement' and the scope of 'non-binding obligation to proceed with negotiations in good faith'. This uncertainty could lead to disputes over contractual obligations if one party decides to terminate negotiations before a final agreement is reached.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party understands and agrees that a preliminary agreement providing for a Possible Transaction is established upon the exchange of Evaluation Material, implying a good faith intention to negotiate a final definitive agreement. Each Party acknowledges that this preliminary agreement creates a non-binding obligation to proceed with negotiations in good faith, subject to the terms and conditions herein. Each Party also agrees that, unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither Party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Possible Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. Neither Party is under any obligation to accept any proposal regarding a Possible Transaction and either Party may terminate discussions and negotiations with the other Party at any time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a 'preliminary agreement' upon the exchange of Evaluation Material, creating a 'non-binding obligation to proceed with negotiations in good faith'. This directly contradicts the original text, which explicitly states that 'no contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists unless and until a final definitive agreement has been executed'. The contradicted text reinforces this by stating that neither party is under any legal obligation until a final agreement is reached, except for matters specifically agreed to herein, further emphasizing the contradiction caused by the changed text's introduction of a preliminary agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party understands and agrees that money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by either Party or any of its Representatives and that the Party against which such breach is committed shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach or threat thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party understands and agrees that money damages shall be the sole and exclusive remedy for any breach of this Agreement by either Party or any of its Representatives. Equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, is hereby waived by both Parties.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by explicitly stating that monetary damages are the sole remedy for breaches of the agreement, while also waiving the right to equitable relief such as injunctions. This directly contradicts the original clause, which states that equitable relief can be sought in addition to monetary damages. Now both parties are in conflict as for which terms to follow.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party understands and agrees that money damages shall be the sole and exclusive remedy for any breach of this Agreement by either Party or any of its Representatives. Equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, is hereby waived by both Parties. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by either Party of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the Party against which such breach is committed.
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text explicitly states that monetary damages are the sole and exclusive remedy and waives equitable relief. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states that monetary damages are the sole and exclusive remedy and waives equitable relief, but then immediately states that such remedies are not the exclusive remedies and are in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity. This creates a clear in-text contradiction as it provides conflicting information in the same section, which aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
